generatedSentence: In a `wealthy`, `neon`, and often `narrow` sector, a celebrated `entertainer` maintained a meticulous `account` of their `clever` `gaming` strategies. Their `laptop` was adorned with `custom` `chintzy` decals, a stark contrast to the `blank` `block` of their `offshore` data. On `weekends`, they would `savor` `leisure` time, `unwound` from the `heckler`s on their `podcast`, often interacting with `lyricists` and `cockney` `arts` enthusiasts who found the `heliocentric` worldview of their digital `books` fascinating, despite the system trying to `outdate` their creative flow. A sudden, `urgent` `battle` began to `harry` the district, a `deadly` `menace` `hitting` with the force of an `electron` pulse, creating `ion` storms. A dedicated `doctor` with a `spine` of steel found himself in a `sprawl` of chaos. His attempts to `filter` the panic were `rebuffs` by the sheer scale of the threat; `killers` moved like `ninjutsu` shadows, `goujons` of energy striking nearby. A colossal robotic `kangaroo`, previously `inactive`, was now animated, its movements `coldly` precise, making it `better` to retreat, lest one `misjudge` its speed. The `calamity` intensified as a `terminator`-like entity, its `eyebrow` raised in an ominous `declaration`, led a horde of `giants` and `barbarians` armed like `samurai` and `viking` warriors. The `visionary` `voice` of the `entertainer`, now a `white`-clad hero with a newfound `superpower`, pierced the din. They harnessed pure `electricity`, a shocking `eclipse` of energy `hitting` the `dragons` that flew overhead. This `luckiest` of `dork`s, now a `jesters` with a `pentagram` of light, moved with `endless` grace through the `layers` of attackers, a `pasta` of destruction unraveling the `imperial` invaders. The `damaging` `dung` of war faded, and the `humanity` of the `jersey` `north` welcomed the `harvest` of peace. The hero, `unstuck` from their past, `hones` their skills. Their `nostril` twitching at the scent of `apricot` blossoms, they found an `idyllic` `seesaw` for the children, while `rangers` cleaned the last `onions` of debris. Though a `weirdo` to some, their `visits` had secured a `bounty` of peace, proving that `elegance` could emerge even from a `haystack` of conflict, as long as `racist` ideologies were kept at bay.

rawPanelSentences: In a `wealthy`, `neon`, and often `narrow` sector, a celebrated `entertainer` maintained a meticulous `account` of their `clever` `gaming` strategies. Their `laptop` was adorned with `custom` `chintzy` decals, a stark contrast to the `blank` `block` of their `offshore` data. On `weekends`, they would `savor` `leisure` time, `unwound` from the `heckler`s on their `podcast`, often interacting with `lyricists` and `cockney` `arts` enthusiasts who found the `heliocentric` worldview of their digital `books` fascinating, despite the system trying to `outdate` their creative flow.

A sudden, `urgent` `battle` began to `harry` the district, a `deadly` `menace` `hitting` with the force of an `electron` pulse, creating `ion` storms. A dedicated `doctor` with a `spine` of steel found himself in a `sprawl` of chaos. His attempts to `filter` the panic were `rebuffs` by the sheer scale of the threat; `killers` moved like `ninjutsu` shadows, `goujons` of energy striking nearby. A colossal robotic `kangaroo`, previously `inactive`, was now animated, its movements `coldly` precise, making it `better` to retreat, lest one `misjudge` its speed.

The `calamity` intensified as a `terminator`-like entity, its `eyebrow` raised in an ominous `declaration`, led a horde of `giants` and `barbarians` armed like `samurai` and `viking` warriors. The `visionary` `voice` of the `entertainer`, now a `white`-clad hero with a newfound `superpower`, pierced the din. They harnessed pure `electricity`, a shocking `eclipse` of energy `hitting` the `dragons` that flew overhead. This `luckiest` of `dork`s, now a `jesters` with a `pentagram` of light, moved with `endless` grace through the `layers` of attackers, a `pasta` of destruction unraveling the `imperial` invaders.

The `damaging` `dung` of war faded, and the `humanity` of the `jersey` `north` welcomed the `harvest` of peace. The hero, `unstuck` from their past, `hones` their skills. Their `nostril` twitching at the scent of `apricot` blossoms, they found an `idyllic` `seesaw` for the children, while `rangers` cleaned the last `onions` of debris. Though a `weirdo` to some, their `visits` had secured a `bounty` of peace, proving that `elegance` could emerge even from a `haystack` of conflict, as long as `racist` ideologies were kept at bay.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a slender figure in a shimmering white bodysuit, with a glowing blue pentagram symbol on their chest, and a futuristic helmet with a single glowing eye. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist on a luxurious hover-deckchair, interacting with a glowing data-screen on a laptop in a narrow, neon-lit room, looking out at a sprawling, wealthy cityscape from above, eye-level shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A colossal robotic kangaroo with inactive glowing red eyes, standing menacingly over a fleeing, panicked crowd in a chaotic city street, seen from a low-angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, now in a dynamic action pose, blasting electricity from their hands towards a flying dragon in a dark, stormy sky during an eclipse, with samurai and viking warriors clashing below, dramatic high-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, now calm and resolute, observing children playing on a seesaw in an idyllic, harvested field with apricot trees, as rangers clean up distant debris, a peaceful wide shot.

definitions.definition: A person who entertains others, esp. as a profession, as a singer, actor, presenter, dancer, musician, magician, comedian, etc.
Someone who puts on a show for the entertainment or enjoyment of others.
The subatomic particle having a negative charge and orbiting the nucleus; the flow of electrons in a conductor constitutes electricity.
Alloys of magnesium and other metals, like aluminum or zinc, that were manufactured by the German company Chemische Fabrik Griesheim-Elektron.
To plunder, pillage, assault.
To make repeated attacks on an enemy.
To strip, lay waste, ravage.
To harass, bother or distress with demands, threats, or criticism.
A rich person.
(with "the") Rich people.
Possessing financial wealth; rich.
Abundant in quality or quantity; profuse.
The chemical element (symbol Ne) with an atomic number of 10. The lightest of the noble gases, it is a colourless, odorless inert gas.
A form or sample of the element.
Neon signs or lights, collectively.
A neon tetra fish.
(of a color) That resembles a neon light; extremely bright; fluorescent
(chiefly in the plural) A narrow passage, especially a contracted part of a stream, lake, or sea; a strait connecting two bodies of water.
definitions.example: the narrows of New York harbor

definitions.definition: Having a small width; not wide; having opposite edges or sides that are close, especially by comparison to length or depth.
definitions.example: a narrow hallway

definitions.definition: Of little extent; very limited; circumscribed.
Restrictive; without flexibility or latitude.
definitions.example: a narrow interpretation

definitions.definition: Contracted; of limited scope; bigoted
definitions.example: a narrow mind

definitions.definition: Having a small margin or degree.
definitions.example: The Republicans won by a narrow majority.

definitions.definition: Limited as to means; straitened
definitions.example: narrow circumstances

definitions.definition: Parsimonious; niggardly; covetous; selfish.
Scrutinizing in detail; close; accurate; exact.
Formed (as a vowel) by a close position of some part of the tongue in relation to the palate; or (according to Bell) by a tense condition of the pharynx; distinguished from wide.
A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.
A sum of money deposited at a bank and subject to withdrawal.
definitions.example: to keep one's account at the bank.

definitions.definition: A statement in general of reasons, causes, grounds, etc., explanatory of some event; a reason of an action to be done.
definitions.example: No satisfactory account has been given of these phenomena.

definitions.definition: A reason, grounds, consideration, motive; a person's sake.
definitions.example: Don't trouble yourself on my account.

definitions.definition: A business relationship involving the exchange of money and credit.
A record of events; recital of transactions; a relation or narrative; a report; a description.
definitions.example: An account of a battle.

definitions.definition: An estimate or estimation; valuation; judgment.
Importance; worth; value; esteem; judgement.
An authorization to use a service.
definitions.example: I've opened an account with Wikipedia so that I can contribute and partake in the project.

definitions.definition: A reckoning; computation; calculation; enumeration; a record of some reckoning.
Profit; advantage.
Nimble with hands or body; skillful; adept.
Resourceful, sometimes to the point of cunning.
definitions.example: clever like a fox

definitions.definition: Smart, intelligent, or witty; mentally quick or sharp.
Showing inventiveness or originality; witty.
(of an Aboriginal Australian) Possessing magical abilities.
Fit; suitable; having propriety.
Well-shaped; handsome.
Good-natured; obliging.
Fit and healthy; free from fatigue or illness.
A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.
definitions.example: a block of ice

definitions.definition: A chopping block; cuboid base for cutting or beheading.
definitions.example: Anne Boleyn placed her head on the block and awaited her execution.

definitions.definition: A group of urban lots of property, several acres in extent, not crossed by public streets.
definitions.example: I'm going for a walk around the block.

definitions.definition: A residential building consisting of flats.
definitions.example: a block of flats

definitions.definition: The distance from one street to another in a city that is built (approximately) to a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The place you are looking for is two long blocks east and one short block north.

definitions.definition: Interference or obstruction of cognitive processes.
The human head.
definitions.example: I'll knock your block off!

definitions.definition: A wig block: a simplified head model upon which wigs are worn.
A mould on which hats, bonnets, etc., are shaped.
A set of sheets (of paper) joined together at one end.
definitions.example: a block of 100 tickets

definitions.definition: A logical data storage unit containing one or more physical sectors (see cluster).
A region of code in a program that acts as a single unit, such as a function or loop.
A fixed-length group of bits making up part of a message.
A case with one or more sheaves/pulleys, used with ropes to increase or redirect force, for example, as part of the rigging of a sailing ship.
A portion of a macromolecule, comprising many units, that has at least one feature not present in adjacent portions.
Something that prevents something from passing.
definitions.example: There's a block in the pipe that means the water can't get through.

definitions.definition: An action to interfere with the movement of an opposing player or of the object of play (ball, puck).
A shot played by holding the bat vertically in the path of the ball, so that it loses momentum and drops to the ground.
A defensive play by one or more players meant to deflect a spiked ball back to the hitter’s court.
A joined group of four (or in some cases nine) postage stamps, forming a roughly square shape.
A section of split logs used as fuel.
Solitary confinement.
A cellblock.
The perch on which a bird of prey is kept.
A piece of hard wood on which a stereotype or electrotype plate is mounted.
A blockhead; a stupid fellow; a dolt.
A section of a railroad where the block system is used.
The position of a player or bat when guarding the wicket.
A blockhole.
The popping crease.
A discrete group of vines in a vineyard, often distinguished from others by variety, clone, canopy training method, irrigation infrastructure, or some combination thereof.
To fill (something) so that it is not possible to pass.
definitions.example: The pipe is blocked.

definitions.definition: To prevent (something or someone) from passing.
definitions.example: You're blocking the road – I can't get through!

definitions.definition: To prevent (something from happening or someone from doing something).
definitions.example: His plan to take over the business was blocked by the boss.

definitions.definition: To impede an opponent.
definitions.example: He blocked the basketball player's shot.

definitions.definition: To specify the positions and movements of the actors.
definitions.example: It was very difficult to block this scene convincingly.

definitions.definition: To hit with a block.
To play a block shot.
To disable communication via telephone, instant messaging, etc., with an undesirable someone.
definitions.example: I tried to send you a message, but you've blocked me!

definitions.definition: To wait.
definitions.example: When the condition expression is false, the thread blocks on the condition variable.

definitions.definition: To stretch or mould (a knitted item, a hat, etc.) into the desired shape.
definitions.example: I blocked the mittens by wetting them and pinning them to a shaped piece of cardboard.

definitions.definition: To shape or sketch out roughly.
A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .
A nonplus .
The white spot in the centre of a target; hence the object to which anything is directed or aimed, the range of such aim .
A lot by which nothing is gained; a ticket in a lottery on which no prize is indicated .
An empty space; a void, for example on a paper .

(now chiefly U.S.) A document, paper, or form with spaces left blank to be filled up at the pleasure of the person to whom it is given (e.g. a blank charter, ballot, form, contract, etc.), or as the event may determine; a blank form .
Blank verse .
A piece of metal (such as a coin, screw, nuts), cut and shaped to the required size of the thing to be made, and ready for the finishing operations; (coining) the disc of metal before stamping .
A vacant space, place, or period; a void .
The 1 / 230400 of a grain .
An empty space in one's memory; a forgotten item or memory .
A dash written in place of an omitted letter or word
The space character; the character resulting from pressing the space-bar on a keyboard.
A domino without points on one or both of its divisions.
definitions.example: the double blank

definitions.definition: Short for blank cartridge. .
(in the expression ‘shooting blanks’) An ineffective effort which achieves nothing .
To make void; to erase.
definitions.example: I blanked out my previous entry.

definitions.definition: To ignore (a person) deliberately.
definitions.example: She blanked me for no reason.

definitions.definition: To prevent from scoring, for example in a sporting event.
definitions.example: England blanks Wales to advance to the final.

definitions.definition: To become blank.
To be temporarily unable to remember.
definitions.example: I'm blanking on her name right now.

definitions.definition: White or pale; without colour.
Free from writing, printing, or marks; having an empty space to be filled in
definitions.example: a blank ballot

definitions.definition: Scoreless; without any goals or points.
Lacking characteristics which give variety; uniform.
definitions.example: a blank desert; a blank wall; blank unconsciousness

definitions.definition: Absolute; downright; sheer.
definitions.example: There was a look of blank terror on his face.

definitions.definition: Without expression.
definitions.example: Failing to understand the question, he gave me a blank stare.

definitions.definition: Utterly confounded or discomfited.
Empty; void; without result; fruitless.
definitions.example: a blank day

definitions.definition: Devoid of thoughts, memory, or inspiration.
definitions.example: The shock left his memory blank.

definitions.definition: Of ammunition: having propellant but no bullets; unbulleted.
definitions.example: The recruits were issued with blank rounds for a training exercise.

definitions.definition: A contest, a struggle.
definitions.example: the battle of life

definitions.definition: A general action, fight, or encounter, in which all the divisions of an army are or may be engaged; a combat, an engagement.
A division of an army; a battalion.
The main body of an army, as distinct from the vanguard and rear; the battalia.
To join in battle; to contend in fight
definitions.example: Scientists always battle over theories.

definitions.definition: To fight or struggle; to enter into a battle with.
definitions.example: She has been battling cancer for years.

definitions.definition: Requiring immediate attention.
definitions.example: An urgent appeal was sent out for assistance.

definitions.definition: A physician; a member of the medical profession; one who is trained and licensed to heal the sick or injured. The final examination and qualification may award a doctor degree in which case the post-nominal letters are D.O., DPM, M.D., DMD, DDS, in the US or MBBS in the UK.
definitions.example: If you still feel unwell tomorrow, see your doctor.

definitions.definition: A person who has attained a doctorate, such as a Ph.D. or Th.D. or one of many other terminal degrees conferred by a college or university.
A veterinarian; a medical practitioner who treats non-human animals.
A nickname for a person who has special knowledge or talents to manipulate or arrange transactions.
A teacher; one skilled in a profession or a branch of knowledge; a learned man.
Any mechanical contrivance intended to remedy a difficulty or serve some purpose in an exigency.
definitions.example: the doctor of a calico-printing machine, which is a knife to remove superfluous colouring matter

definitions.definition: A fish, the friar skate.
To act as a medical doctor to.
definitions.example: Her children doctored her back to health.

definitions.definition: To act as a medical doctor.
To make (someone) into an (academic) doctor; to confer a doctorate upon.
To physically alter (medically or surgically) a living being in order to change growth or behavior.
definitions.example: They doctored their apple trees by vigorous pruning, and now the dwarfed trees are easier to pick.

definitions.definition: To genetically alter an extant species.
definitions.example: Mendel's discoveries showed how the evolution of a species may be doctored.

definitions.definition: To alter or make obscure, as with the intention to deceive, especially a document.
To take medicine.
The series of bones situated at the back from the head to the pelvis of a person, or from the head to the tail of an animal; backbone, vertebral column.
Something resembling a backbone, such as a ridge, or a long, central structure from which other structures radiate.
A pointed, fairly rigid protuberance or needle-like structure on an animal, shell, or plant.
The heartwood of trees.
Courage or assertiveness.
The stiffness of an arrow.
An ungainly sprawling posture.
A straggling, haphazard growth, especially of housing on the edge of a city.
To sit with the limbs spread out.
To spread out in a disorderly fashion; to straggle.
A small tailless amphibian of the order Anura that typically hops.
The part of a violin bow (or that of other similar string instruments such as the viola, cello and contrabass) located at the end held by the player, to which the horsehair is attached.
Road. Shorter, more common form of frog and toad.
The depression in the upper face of a pressed or handmade clay brick.
An organ on the bottom of a horse’s hoof that assists in the circulation of blood.
The part of a railway switch or turnout where the running-rails cross (from the resemblance to the frog in a horse’s hoof).
To hunt or trap frogs.
To use a pronged plater to transfer (cells) to another plate.
To spatchcock (a chicken).
A French person.
A French-speaking person from Quebec.
A leather or fabric loop used to attach a sword or bayonet, or its scabbard, to a waist or shoulder belt.
An ornate fastener for clothing consisting of an oblong button (covered with netted thread), toggle, or knot, that fits through a loop.
To ornament or fasten a coat, etc. with frogs.
To unravel part of (a knitted garment) while knitting it in order to correct a mistake.
A device which separates a suspended, dissolved, or particulate matter from a fluid, solution, or other substance; any device that separates one substance from another.
Electronics or software that separates unwanted signals (for example noise) from wanted signals or that attenuates selected frequencies.
Any item, mechanism, device or procedure that acts to separate or isolate.
definitions.example: He runs an email filter to catch the junk mail.

definitions.definition: Self-restraint in speech.
definitions.example: He's got no filter, and he's always offending people as a result.

definitions.definition: A non-empty upper set (of a partially ordered set) which is closed under binary infima (a.k.a. meets).
definitions.example: If (1) the universal set (here, the set of natural numbers) were called a "large" set, (2) the superset of any "large" set were also a "large" set, and (3) the intersection of a pair of "large" sets were also a "large" set, then the set of all "large" sets would form a filter.

definitions.definition: To sort, sift, or isolate.
definitions.example: This strainer should filter out the large particles.

definitions.definition: To diffuse; to cause to be less concentrated or focused.
definitions.example: The leaves of the trees filtered the light.

definitions.definition: To pass through a filter or to act as though passing through a filter.
definitions.example: The water filtered through the rock and soil.

definitions.definition: To move slowly or gradually; to come or go a few at a time.
definitions.example: The crowd filtered into the theater.

definitions.definition: To ride a motorcycle between lanes on a road
definitions.example: I can skip past all the traffic on my bike by filtering.

definitions.definition: The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses and emotions, usually specifically the production of the beautiful in a graphic or plastic medium.
definitions.example: There is a debate as to whether graffiti is art or vandalism.

definitions.definition: The creative and emotional expression of mental imagery, such as visual, auditory, social, etc.
Skillful creative activity, usually with an aesthetic focus.
definitions.example: She's mastered the art of programming.

definitions.definition: The study and the product of these processes.
definitions.example: He's at university to study art.

definitions.definition: Aesthetic value.
definitions.example: Her photographs are nice, but there's no art in them.

definitions.definition: Artwork.
definitions.example: Sotheby's regularly auctions art for millions.

definitions.definition: A field or category of art, such as painting, sculpture, music, ballet, or literature.
definitions.example: I'm a great supporter of the arts.

definitions.definition: A nonscientific branch of learning; one of the liberal arts.
Skill that is attained by study, practice, or observation.
Contrivance, scheming, manipulation.
The humanities.
The liberal arts.
Material, made from the polished skin of a calf, sheep, goat or other animal, used like paper for writing.
A document made on such material.
A diploma (traditionally written on parchment).
Stiff paper imitating that material.
The creamy to tanned color of parchment.
The envelope of the coffee grains, inside the pulp.
(plural: reindeer) Any Arctic and subarctic-dwelling deer of the species Rangifer tarandus, with a number of subspecies.
(plural: reindeers) Any species, subspecies, ecotype, or other scientific grouping of such animals.
A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.
definitions.example: He was frustrated because he couldn't find anything about dinosaurs in the book.

definitions.definition: A long work fit for publication, typically prose, such as a novel or textbook, and typically published as such a bound collection of sheets, but now sometimes electronically as an e-book.
definitions.example: I have three copies of his first book.

definitions.definition: A major division of a long work.
definitions.example: Genesis is the first book of the Bible.

definitions.definition: A record of betting (from the use of a notebook to record what each person has bet).
definitions.example: I'm running a book on who is going to win the race.

definitions.definition: A convenient collection, in a form resembling a book, of small paper items for individual use.
definitions.example: a book of raffle tickets

definitions.definition: The script of a musical or opera.
(usually in the plural) Records of the accounts of a business.
A book award, a recognition for receiving the highest grade in a class (traditionally an actual book, but recently more likely a letter or certificate acknowledging the achievement).
(whist) Six tricks taken by one side.
Four of a kind
A document, held by the referee, of the incidents happened in the game.
(by extension) A list of all players who have been booked (received a warning) in a game.
The twenty-sixth Lenormand card.
Any source of instruction.
To reserve (something) for future use.
definitions.example: I can book tickets for the concert next week.

definitions.definition: To write down, to register or record in a book or as in a book.
definitions.example: They booked that message from the hill

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) To record the name and other details of a suspected offender and the offence for later judicial action.
definitions.example: The police booked him for driving too fast.

definitions.definition: To issue with a caution, usually a yellow card, or a red card if a yellow card has already been issued.
To travel very fast.
definitions.example: He was really booking, until he passed the speed trap.

definitions.definition: To record bets as bookmaker.
(law student slang) To receive the highest grade in a class.
definitions.example: The top three students had a bet on which one was going to book their intellectual property class.

definitions.definition: To leave.
definitions.example: He was here earlier, but he booked.

definitions.definition: A folding chair in which a wooden frame supports a length of canvas; traditionally used on the beach or onboard a liner.
Grace, refinement, and beauty in movement, appearance, or manners.
definitions.example: The bride was elegance personified.

definitions.definition: Restraint and grace of style.
definitions.example: The simple dress had a quiet elegance.

definitions.definition: The beauty of an idea characterized by minimalism and intuitiveness while preserving exactness and precision.
definitions.example: The proof of the theorem had a pleasing elegance.

definitions.definition: A refinement or luxury.
A sharpening stone composed of extra-fine grit used for removing the burr or curl from the blade of a razor or some other edge tool.
A machine tool used in the manufacture of precision bores.
To sharpen with a hone; to whet.
To use a hone to produce a precision bore.
To refine or master (a skill).
To make more acute, intense, or effective.
A kind of swelling in the cheek.
To grumble.
To pine, lament, or long.
A mound, pile, or stack of stored hay.
(canoeing) A standing wave in a rapid.
The text string within which another string is searched for. (see: needle in a haystack)
A dish composed of a starchy food (rice, tortillas, crackers, etc.) topped by a protein (beans, cheese, meat, etc.) in combination with fresh vegetables, assembled on the plate by the diner.
(of people) Favoured by luck; fortunate; having good success or good fortune

definitions.definition: The shape of a five-pointed star constructed of five intersecting lines meeting at the vertices, such that a central pentagon and five surrounding isosceles triangles are formed; often with magical connotations; a 5/2 (or 5/3) star polygon.

definitions.definition: That which causes stress or is extremely difficult, especially that which may cause failure at a task.

definitions.definition: Something that is so far ahead of its competition that it effectively kills off that competition.

definitions.definition: A knockout form of darts or pool involving several players.

A particularly heavy type of handstamp, or portion of one, often obscuring a large part of the postage stamp.

A diacritical mark used in Indic scripts to suppress an inherent vowel (e.g., the Hindi viram, the Bengali or Oriya hasanta) or render the entire syllable silent (e.g., the Burmese virama, the Khmer toandakhiat).
A sea mammal related to dolphins and porpoises (Orcinus orca).
Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.
An abrupt rapping sound, as from an impact of a hard object against wood.

definitions.definition: Criticism.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.

The study of electrical phenomena; the branch of science dealing with such phenomena.
A feeling of excitement; a thrill.
definitions.example: Opening night for the new production had an electricity unlike other openings.

definitions.definition: Electric power/energy as used in homes etc., supplied by power stations or generators.
An alignment of astronomical objects whereby one object comes between the observer (or notional observer) and another object, thus obscuring the latter.

Especially, an alignment whereby a planetary object (for example, the Moon) comes between the Sun and another planetary object (for example, the Earth), resulting in a shadow being cast by the middle planetary object onto the other planetary object.
A seasonal state of plumage in some birds, notably ducks, adopted temporarily after the breeding season and characterised by a dull and scruffy appearance.
Obscurity, decline, downfall
Of astronomical bodies, to cause an eclipse.
definitions.example: The Moon eclipsed the Sun.

definitions.definition: To overshadow; to be better or more noticeable than.
(Irish grammar) To undergo eclipsis.
A mythical human of very great size.
Specifically, any of the gigantes, the race of giants in the Greek mythology.
A very tall and large person.
A tall species of a particular animal or plant.
A star that is considerably more luminous than a main sequence star of the same temperature (e.g. red giant, blue giant).
An Ethernet packet that exceeds the medium's maximum packet size of 1,518 bytes.
A very large organisation.
definitions.example: The retail giant is set to acquire two more struggling high-street chains.

definitions.definition: A person of extraordinary strength or powers, bodily or intellectual.
Jotun
In feudal Japan, a soldier who served a daimyo.
